title: Echinocactus crassihamatus
intro: Echinocactus crassihamatus, commonly known as the Prickly Pear, is a type of cactus native to the Americas, characterized by its distinctive rounded stems covered in sharp spines. The Prickly Pear has been traditionally used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of constipation, as well as to support wound healing due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinocactus crassihamatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from the Prickly Pear include ointments, creams, and tinctures, which are used to treat skin irritations and promote wound healing.

It is also known as a species of "golden barrel cactus" due to its golden spines.
